hyperedges.label: Four-Scanner Suite over a Shared Directory Walk

nodes.label: Airgap Guardian
nodes.rationale: Offline-first security tool for air-gapped environments: a single CLI executable requiring no network access, so it can run where connectivity is deliberately absent.

nodes.label: Certificate Scanner (cert)
nodes.rationale: Scans X.509 certificates (PEM/DER auto-detected) for expiration and security issues; unparseable files become parse errors instead of aborting the scan.

nodes.label: SSH Scanner
nodes.rationale: Uses format heuristics (OpenSSH cipher name, PEM Proc-Type headers) rather than decryption to detect passphrase protection, keeping the scan offline and passive.

nodes.label: Secrets Scanner
nodes.rationale: Conservative regex rules to limit false positives; binary files and files over 1 MiB skipped for performance; previews redacted so secrets are never printed in full.

nodes.label: JWT Scanner
nodes.rationale: Analyzes structure and claims (alg, exp, iss, aud) without signature verification, since key material is unavailable offline; non-JSON candidates silently ignored to avoid noise.

nodes.label: Policy Engine
nodes.rationale: All thresholds resolved from one policy loaded and validated before scanning, so a report can always be interpreted from its embedded policy alone; unknown TOML fields are rejected to catch typos; invalid policies fail fast with exit code 7.

nodes.label: Risk Score
nodes.rationale: Additive 0-100 score from expiration status and findings, capped at 100, giving a single comparable severity number across certificates and assets.

nodes.label: HTML Report
nodes.rationale: Standalone single file with embedded CSS, no JavaScript, no external assets so it renders fully offline; the active policy is embedded so reports remain interpretable later without the original policy file.

nodes.label: JSON Output
nodes.rationale: Confirmation messages go to stderr so JSON on stdout stays clean and pipeable; every entry carries an asset_type and the effective policy is embedded in the output.

nodes.label: scan Command
nodes.rationale: Takes exactly one input (positional directory or --inventory); --scanners cannot combine with --inventory because the inventory specifies scanners per target.

nodes.label: discover Command
nodes.rationale: Metadata-first classification (file names, extensions, directory names) instead of full analysis so large trees can be surveyed quickly; only small text files are probed for JWT structures.

nodes.label: inventory Command
nodes.rationale: Catalogs every discovered asset with complete details rather than reporting findings; exits 0 regardless of findings because cataloging is not a pass/fail check.

nodes.label: Inventory File (inventory.toml)
nodes.rationale: Versioned TOML with [[scan]] entries; duplicate paths merged and scanner lists combined so each unique directory is walked exactly once; strict validation (exit code 7) reports all violations in a single message.

nodes.label: Exit Codes
nodes.rationale: scan returns the highest severity encountered (0-3) so scripts can gate on results; 4-7 are reserved for usage, missing directory, runtime, and policy/inventory errors.

nodes.label: Security Check Rules
nodes.rationale: Findings are reported separately from expiration status; asset checks reuse the same policy thresholds as certificate checks for consistency.

nodes.label: Single Shared Directory Walk
nodes.rationale: All scanners share one directory walk; each file is read at most once and offered to every scanner that can process it; a failure in one file or scanner never aborts the scan — performance plus resilience.

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph Workflow
nodes.rationale: query/path/explain return a scoped subgraph much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep output; graphify update is AST-only with no API cost, so the graph stays current cheaply after code changes.

hyperedges.label: Four-Scanner Suite over a Shared Directory Walk
